title: Trivia — Tnufa A2 vocabulary + chunks
subtitle: 10 multiple-choice questions on Band 2 chunks and A2 grammar (Track B-C)

items.text: Choose the chunk that means "to give a reason":
items.explanation: "Because of" introduces a cause. "In order to" expresses purpose. "On the other hand" introduces contrast. "As a result" introduces effect.

items.text: Which sentence uses "in order to" correctly?
options: I went home in order to.
She studies in order to pass the exam.
He runs in order to fast.
items.explanation: "In order to" + base verb = purpose/intention. "She studies IN ORDER TO PASS the exam" means the goal of studying is to pass.
"Good" is irregular: comparative is "better", superlative is "best". "Gooder" doesn't exist.

items.text: Choose the chunk for expressing opinion:
items.explanation: "In my opinion" introduces a personal view. "According to" cites a source. "For example" gives an instance. "As well" means "also".

items.text: Which adjective is in the SUPERLATIVE form?
items.explanation: Superlative for short adjectives: "the" + adjective + -est. "The tallest" = highest of all. "Taller" is comparative (2 items).

items.text: What does "on the other hand" express?
items.explanation: "On the other hand" introduces a contrasting perspective. "On one hand X, on the other hand Y" — used to present opposing views.

items.text: Choose the correct chunk: "___ the report, sales went up 20%."
items.explanation: "According to + source" cites where the information comes from. "According to the report" = the report says so.
Present Perfect: HAVE/HAS + past participle. "Have you ever VISITED London?" The past participle of "visit" is "visited" (regular verb).

items.text: Which sentence is in the most appropriate register for a friendly letter?
options: Dear Sir or Madam, I am writing to inform you...
Hi Sam! How are you? I miss you!
items.explanation: Friendly letter = informal register. "Hi Sam! How are you?" is casual and warm. The others are formal business register.

items.text: What is the integrating reading skill?
items.explanation: "Integrating ideas" means combining information from 2 or more sources/paragraphs to form a complete picture. It's THE new skill added in Grade 8.
